title: ServerKit Manifest
description: Declarative deployment manifest (serverkit.yaml / serverkit.json). A version:1 file describes a whole deployment — services, databases, disks, backups, env vars, cron and domains — that ServerKit parses, diffs against live state, and applies. camelCase is canonical; snake_case aliases are accepted everywhere.

version.description: Manifest schema version. 1 selects the declarative multi-service spec. Files without version:1 are legacy flat manifests and keep their v0 behavior.

server.description: Optional default fleet target (agent server name or id). A service may override it. Omit for the local panel host.

project.description: Optional project name this manifest maps to. Defaults to the repository name.

envVars.description: Manifest-level (project-scoped) environment variables, applied to every service as a shared variable group.

services.description: The services that make up this deployment.

name.description: Unique service name within the manifest. Becomes the Application (or ManagedDatabase) name.

type.description: Service kind. web/worker/static/docker create Applications; postgres/mysql/mariadb/redis create ManagedDatabases.

runtime.description: Build runtime hint for app services (e.g. python, node, static, docker). Ignored for db types.

buildCommand.description: Command used to build the app (buildpack override).

startCommand.description: Command used to start the app (buildpack override).

port.description: Container port the app listens on.

ports.description: Typed L4 port publishes (appliance tier). Each entry is a raw tcp/udp publish — the escape hatch for services that speak more than HTTP (media/UDP, VoIP, brokers). The legacy scalar `port` keeps its HTTP/nginx behavior and is separate.

image.description: Container image reference for a BYO-image app (appliance tier) — a first-class peer of the buildpack path.

registry.description: Named private registry to authenticate against before pulling `image`. A registry that is unknown or has no stored credential is a plan-time blocker.

hostRequirements.description: Elevated host requirements (appliance tier) — privileged/capAdd/sysctls/devices/kernelModules. Always listed in plain words in the plan; never applied silently.

bootstrap.description: First-boot bootstrap (appliance tier). Runs once via `docker compose run --rm` after volumes exist and before the first up — for appliances that generate a config tree once.

containers.description: A multi-container UNIT (appliance tier): one service rendered as one compose project with a private network and health-gated dependsOn. Mutually exclusive with the buildpack keys. Keyed by container name.

healthCheckPath.description: HTTP path used for health checks and the zero-downtime restart gate.

autoDeploy.description: When true, a push that changes this service's declared config auto-applies. Otherwise the manifest flips to pending and waits for an explicit apply.

version.description: Engine version for db services (e.g. "16" for postgres).

disk.description: Single primary disk for db services.

cron.description: Scheduled job(s) run in the service context.

command.description: Command run once inside a throwaway service container.

timeoutSeconds.description: Max seconds the bootstrap may run before it is killed and the step fails.

registry.description: Named private registry to authenticate against before pulling.

httpPath.description: HTTP path probed with wget on localhost.

service.description: Sibling container name in the same unit.

condition.description: healthy waits for the dependency's healthcheck; started only waits for it to start.

privileged.description: Run privileged (big hammer — always shown in the plan, never silent).

kernelModules.description: Kernel modules the workload needs (advisory /proc/modules check only).

containerPort.description: Container port to publish to. Defaults to the host port.

expose.description: public = bind 0.0.0.0 and open the firewall; local = bind 127.0.0.1 only. Defaults to public.

value.description: Literal value. Use only for non-secrets — secrets never live in the manifest.

fromSecret.description: Reference to a vault secret by name; resolved at injection time, never stored in the env row.

fromService.description: Reference to a sibling service's connection property.

fromServer.description: Reference to the service's own target server identity (appliance tier). publicIp is what a NAT'd media/WebRTC bridge must advertise to clients.

generate.description: When true, ServerKit generates a random value on first apply, stores it as a vault secret, and rewires this entry as a fromSecret reference.

size.description: Requested size (e.g. 10GB). Recorded; not enforced by the local driver.

service.description: Name of the service this domain routes to.

ssl.description: Certificate mode. auto = best-effort issuance (never blocks); off = HTTP only.
